Table 3: Retrieval accuracy of image-to-text (I→T) and text-to-image (T→I) retrieval.
Figure 2: (a) 𝑘-NN classification accuracy and (b) I→T retrieval accuracy of three baselines and DP-GCL during training.

Figure 1: Overview of DP-GCL. Given 𝐵 data pairs (𝑥𝑖 , 𝑥+𝑖 ), we partition them into ⌈𝐵/𝑆⌉ disjoint groups. Within each group, every sample is only contrasted against other samples from the same group, and comparisons across different groups are explicitly discarded. We apply data augmentation within each group to generate diverse samples for comparison. The contrastive loss is then computed independently for each group. Each gradient is clipped individually and aggregated with Gaussian noise for DP guarantee. For uni-modal contrastive learning, the parameters of E and E+ are shared.
Figure 4: I→T retrieval accuracy (first row) and gradient Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) of gradients (second row) with different group sizes.
Figure 3: Image-to-text (I→T) and text-to-image (T→I) retrieval accuracy of three baselines and DP-GCL under different batch sizes {1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128} × 16.
Figure 7: Example image-text pairs from four investigated multi-modal datasets: (a) PEDES, (b) RSTPReid, (c) Fashion, and (d) ROCO
